summary: Repository-wide fighter naming cleanup replacing the parameter/local name `fighterObj` with `fighter_gobj` across fighter core code, fighter headers, and many character-specific files. The PR explicitly resolved issue #524 and was a zero-net-line refactor in the shown changed-file counts: 2722 added and 2722 deleted, indicating mostly mechanical renaming rather than behavior changes.
what_changed: Renamed `fighterObj` parameters to `fighter_gobj` in public declarations such as `Fighter_ActionStateChange_800693AC`, `func_8006EBE8`, `func_8006F0FC`, `func_8007AF28`, `func_800C0A98`, `func_800D3158`, `func_800DEEA8`, and `ftSamus_80128AC8` in `include/functions.h`.
Renamed matching function parameters and all in-body uses in fighter implementation files, including `src/melee/ft/fighter.c`, `fighter.h`, `ft_unknown_006.c`, `ftwalkcommon.c`, and related headers.
Applied the same naming convention across many character modules, including Donkey Kong, Pikachu, Samus, Zelda, Link, Kirby, Purin, Yoshi, Sheik, Mewtwo, Luigi, Ice Climbers, and others.

Examples from the diff show direct mechanical substitutions such as `fighterObj->user_data` becoming `fighter_gobj->user_data`, `getFighter(fighterObj)` becoming `getFighter(fighter_gobj)`, and calls like `Fighter_OnItemPickup(fighterObj, ...)` becoming `Fighter_OnItemPickup(fighter_gobj, ...)`.

The diff excerpt also shows file mode changes for `build.sh`, `gen.sh`, and `resync.sh` from `100644` to `100755`, but the provided changed-file list and PR title/body focus on the fighter object rename.
smart_moves: The rename was applied consistently to both declarations and definitions, preventing header/implementation drift for function prototypes that name the first fighter object parameter.
The chosen name `fighter_gobj` preserves the semantic distinction that the variable is an `HSD_GObj*` representing a fighter, not the `Fighter*` user-data struct itself.
Keeping the change mechanical and line-balanced made review safer: evidence shows equal added/deleted lines and no indicated logic edits in the excerpt.
Applying the convention repository-wide avoided a mixed-style transition where new decomp work would have to choose between `fighterObj` and `fighter_gobj`.
decomp_lessons: For Melee fighter code, prefer names that encode both domain and type: `fighter_gobj` for an `HSD_GObj*` fighter object, and reserve `fp`/`Fighter*` style names for the actual fighter user-data pointer.

When renaming a common parameter used across many matched functions, update headers and prototypes in the same PR as implementation bodies to preserve call-site clarity and reduce future merge conflicts.

Mechanical naming-only PRs are useful in decomp projects because they improve readability without perturbing generated code or matching assumptions, provided no expression structure changes are introduced.

A consistent object-pointer naming convention helps future decompers distinguish GObj handles from game-specific structs when reading patterns like `fighter_gobj->user_data`, `getFighter(fighter_gobj)`, and `Fighter_ActionStateChange_800693AC(fighter_gobj, ...)`.

naming_conventions.meaning: An `HSD_GObj*` whose user_data is a fighter; the suffix `_gobj` makes the GObj layer explicit.

naming_conventions.pattern: Use snake_case for descriptive parameter names in fighter code when the type role matters.
naming_conventions.evidence: The PR title and diff systematically replace camelCase-ish `fighterObj` with snake_case `fighter_gobj`.

naming_conventions.pattern: Keep `fp` for `Fighter*` locals obtained from `fighter_gobj->user_data`, `getFighter(fighter_gobj)`, or `getFighterPlus(fighter_gobj)`.
naming_conventions.evidence: Diff examples retain `Fighter* fp` while only renaming the `HSD_GObj*` variable.

assembly_or_matching_tactics: This was a source naming cleanup with no direct assembly-matching tactic evident from the supplied PR slice.
The equal added/deleted line counts and direct substitutions suggest the intent was to avoid behavior or codegen changes while improving naming consistency.
Future large mechanical renames should remain expression-preserving: only rename identifiers, leaving call order, casts, temporaries, literals, and control flow untouched.
review_feedback: No issue comments or review comments were included in the PR context slice.
The only human body text shown was `Resolves #524.`
There was one review recorded in the counts, but no review text or reviewer rationale was available in the provided excerpt.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that use or extend the `fighter_gobj` naming convention.
